While all other elements (title, journal, date, volume, article ID) are individually plausible and consistent with a real BMJ article from September 2021, the author name defect compromises the reference's integrity. In academic citation validation, a single critical error (like a fabricated author name) renders the entire reference unreliable, as it prevents verification and suggests possible fabrication or severe corruption.
